prompt_text: Teamtailor vs Workable pricing and features
response:
**Teamtailor** is generally positioned as a more *custom-quoted* ATS with strong employer branding and collaboration features, while **Workable** has more transparent public pricing, broader sourcing/job distribution, and more HR-adjacent functionality.[1][2][4]

| Category | Teamtailor | Workable |
|---|---|---|
| **Pricing** | No public pricing listed; customized pricing based on business requirements.[2][3] | Public entry-level pricing is available; one source lists a starting point of **$249/month** for the Starter plan, while another comparison page shows **$359/month** and notes annual discounts on the Standard plan.[1][2] |
| **Free trial** | Available.[3] | Available; G2 says a 15-day trial with no credit card is offered.[3] |
| **Core strengths** | Applicant tracking, employer branding, collaboration, customizable reporting, job templates, scorecards, tagging, and interview kits.[2] | 200+ job boards, AI-powered sourcing/recommendations, customizable workflows, reporting/analytics, mobile app, and HR features like payroll/time tracking/onboarding in broader product positioning.[2][4] |
| **Best fit** | Teams prioritizing a simple, collaborative hiring workflow and employer branding.[2] | Teams prioritizing high-volume hiring, sourcing, job distribution, and broader HR capabilities.[2][4] |

**Feature-wise, Teamtailor emphasizes the candidate experience and internal collaboration**, with tools such as job templates, scorecards, tagging, employee dashboards, and customizable reporting.[2] **Workable emphasizes reach and automation**, including posting to 200+ job boards, AI sourcing, built-in interview scheduling, advanced analytics, and mobile collaboration.[2][4]

On user ratings, both are highly rated on comparison sites, but G2 shows **Teamtailor at 4.6/5** and **Workable at 4.5/5** from more reviews for Workable.[3] TrustRadius also shows very close ratings for enterprise and mid-market segments, with Workable slightly higher for small and medium businesses in that comparison.[1]

If you want, I can also give you a **“which one should I pick?” recommendation** based on your team size, hiring volume, and budget.
